content.problemStatement:
Find the length of the longest contiguous segment in an array, in which if a given element KKK is inserted, KKK becomes the second largest element of that subarray.

Input:
The first line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and KKK.
The next line contains N space-separated integers Ai denoting the elements of the array.
Output:Print a single line corresponding to each test case — the length of the largest segment.
constraints: Sum of N across all test cases doesn't exceed 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of test cases T. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and K, where N is the number of elements in the array and K is the given element. The second line contains N space-separated integers denoting the elements of the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the length of the longest contiguous segment in which if the given element K is inserted, K becomes the second largest element of that subarray.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, if 3 is inserted anywhere in the array, it is the second largest element. Hence the maximum length is 5. For the second test case, if 5 is inserted anywhere between the 1st and 4th element, it is the second largest element. The length of such subarray is 3.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a sliding window approach. We maintain two pointers, left and right, representing the current window. We also keep track of the maximum and second maximum elements in the current window. We iterate over the array, expanding the window to the right and updating the maximum and second maximum elements. If the given element K is greater than the maximum element in the current window, we move the left pointer to the right until K is not greater than the maximum element. If K is less than or equal to the maximum element, we update the maximum and second maximum elements and move the right pointer to the right. We keep track of the maximum length of the window in which K is the second largest element.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the maximum number of elements in the array. The outer loop iterates over each test case, and the inner loop iterates over each element in the array.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we only use a constant amount of space to store the pointers, maximum and second maximum elements, and the maximum length of the window.
